title: Dog is my Copilot
name: Dog is my Copilot (DIMCP)
summary:
"Dog is my Copilot" (abbreviated DIMCP) is a long-form episodic video series Colin created
in 2018. It's a food delivery vlog featuring recurring characters — including a co-host
named "Tracy" — filmed during actual delivery runs. The series spans at least two seasons
and 96+ numbered episodes (EP_01 through EP_96), archived as MP4 files under
_Documentation/Videos/2018/Dog is my Copilot/. One of the most prolific single creative
projects in the archive by file count (130+ video files).
body:
# Dog is my Copilot (DIMCP)

One of Colin's most prolific late-era creative projects. In 2018, he created and self-produced a long-running episodic video series while working food delivery. The show is raw and documentary in register — filmed from the vehicle during deliveries, featuring a recurring co-host named "Tracy" and the kind of off-the-cuff commentary, humor, and observation that characterizes Colin's voice across mediums.

The transcript for EP_01 opens with the premise stated plainly: *"It's a show where two badass bad bitches deliver food."* The vlog runs through the actual texture of the delivery shift — routes, customers, conversations, asides — while establishing an ongoing cast and running jokes between the hosts.

By file count, DIMCP may be the single largest creative project in the archive. 130+ MP4 files spread across at least two seasons (Season 1 and Season 2 folders), with episodes numbered EP_01 through at least EP_96. Each episode presumably corresponds to a delivery shift or partial shift.

## Format & Structure

- **Medium**: Video (MP4)
- **Format**: Episodic vlog; filmed during food delivery runs
- **Hosts**: Colin Ward + "Tracy" (recurring co-host, full name unconfirmed)
- **Seasons**: At least 2 (Season 1 and Season 2 archive folders confirmed)
- **Episode count**: 96+ confirmed (EP_01–EP_96 in archive)
- **File count**: 130+ MP4 files total

## Context

DIMCP represents a significant shift in medium relative to Colin's music projects. While alphabets and killd by are purely audio, DIMCP is video-native — a vlog format that anticipates the creator-economy idiom even as it stays entirely in Colin's DIY, unpolished aesthetic. The fact that he sustained it for 96+ episodes in a single year suggests it was a genuine ongoing practice, not a one-off experiment.

The series title — "Dog is my Copilot" — riffs on the "God is my Copilot" phrase, grounding it in the secular and the canine (or possibly ironic). Whether a dog appears on-screen is unconfirmed from transcripts reviewed.

## Archive Notes

- Location: `_Documentation/Videos/2018/Dog is my Copilot/`
- Season folders: Season 1, Season 2
- Transcript .txt files present for some episodes (EP_01 confirmed)
- No known public upload location; assumed private/unreleased
- Created in the same year as Colin's death (October 2018) — the series may be incomplete
